feedback.calculationMethod: Mini report holistic evaluation: You provide multiple concrete examples of ATL skillsâself-management, research, social and communication skillsâthat clearly contributed to your projectâs development. To deepen this section, link each ATL skill explicitly to specific actions or results and expand on your problem-solving steps during technical challenges.

feedback.calculationMethod: Mini report holistic evaluation: Your planning demonstrates strong alignment of pedagogical goals with game mechanics and context awareness of platform constraints. However, the plan would be strengthened by defining specific, measurable success criteria, fully mapping ATL skills to planned actions, and including a detailed schedule with task dependencies and resource allocations to ensure feasibility and track progress.

strengths: Insightful reflection connecting project experience to future studies and career ambitions
Integration of supervisor feedback into adaptive learning features and optimization
Clear, prioritized list of next steps for improvements
Deep personal growth reflection on risk-taking and balancing fun with learning
weaknesses: Reflection lacks explicit evaluation against each success criterion

feedback.calculationMethod: Mini report holistic evaluation: Your reflection captures personal growth, future application, and a forward-looking improvement plan, demonstrating strong metacognitive awareness. To enhance this section, evaluate your product rigorously against each predefined success criterion and support your analysis with specific evidence such as playtest metrics or user feedback.

annotations.comment: Communication section relies on vague list items and appendix references. Embed a specific email excerpt or meeting note to demonstrate direct ATL application.
Your Trello board screenshot clearly shows effective self-management and task organization. Visual evidence like this strengthens your Applying Skills narrative.
Excellent use of thinking skills: you interviewed a subject-matter expert to refine your wireframe and game design principles, showing proactive problem-solving.
Your social skills reflection demonstrates conflict resolution via stakeholder discussion and phased development. This shows strong collaboration and adaptability.
Your reflecting introduction outlines growth but lacks an explicit evaluation against each success criterion. Link reflections directly to your measurable targets to deepen analysis.
Your evaluation against success criteria lists outcomes but doesnât provide specific evidence (e.g., playtest data). Include quantitative or qualitative feedback to justify each point.
Your final reflection on how this experience shapes your future studies and career ambitions is insightful, clearly linking project outcomes to ongoing personal development.
Meeting notes show you integrated supervisor feedback into adaptive learning features and optimizationâthis demonstrates reflective practice and continuous improvement.
Embedding adaptive difficulty and reward mechanics directly aligns your intended product with student learning outcomesâthis is a well-considered plan.
You illustrate communication skills by preparing meeting notes and questions beforehand, which made interactions more productive. This concrete strategy strengthens your Applying Skills.
Self-management reflection shows you learned to allocate buffer time after underestimating tasks. This demonstrates effective problem-solving in your workflow.
Your improvements list offers clear, prioritized next steps (animations, compression, optimization). This forward-looking plan strengthens your reflective practice.
Your introduction to Criterion B outlines ATL skills but doesnât give specific instances of their application. Link each skill to a concrete action or outcome in your project.
Strong reflection on personal growthârisk-taking in coding and balancing fun with learning shows clear insight into how your self-management and thinking skills advanced.
Excellent integration of environmental themes with grammar learning. Embedding exercises in real-world scenarios enhances engagement and educational impact.

You present a clear, engaging learning goal with strong personal interest and wellâdefined context for your 3D grammar game. This establishes a solid foundation for your projectâs purpose and relevance.

You describe your product goal but lack measurable success criteria here. Add specific, quantifiable targets (e.g., completion of X levels, <Y> minutes of playtesting feedback) to strengthen your plan.
You reference ATL creativity as critical but your plan doesnât show how other ATL skills will support development. Outline roles for research, collaboration, and self-management in your planning.
Good analysis of hardware constraints and intended platform. This contextual awareness ensures your game remains accessible in your target school environment.
Your thinking skills discussion flags optimization challenges but stops short of detailing how you resolved them. Add a step-by-step breakdown of your troubleshooting process for clarity.
Your âSuccess criteriaâ heading is clear, but criteria need detail. Specify metrics (e.g., player accuracy â¥80%, load time <5s) to make evaluation objective.
Your productâfeature table outlines status but lacks timeline sequencing and resource allocation. Add a Gantt chart or schedule notes to demonstrate feasibility.
Task durations are provided, but dependencies and deadlines arenât clear. Include task sequencing and milestones to show how your plan will stay on track.
Strong use of research skills: you evaluated credible sources and synthesized game design insights effectively, balancing educational goals with technical feasibility.
Good reflection on time management limits; you identify over-researching and set boundaries. This shows self-management awareness that improved your efficiency.
